TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Darkborn Elves Faction.

Translations.Comment: Dunkelgeboren = darkborn. Dark elves with a sinister edge. 'Natos' would be shorter alternative.
Spear-wielding dark elf infantry. Concise and martial.
Schrecken = dread. Elite heavy infantry radiating fear. Conveys menace and military hierarchy.
Hexen = witches. Dark magic users of the dark elves. Classic and evocative.
Crossbow-wielding dark elves. Deadly ranged troops. Professional military terminology.
Schatten = shadows. Stealthy infiltrators and assassins. Evokes stealth and darkness.
Heavy artillery. Consistent with other bolt thrower translations. Ballista is standard Spanish term for siege weapon.
Knights riding vicious raptors. Fast and deadly cavalry. Concise; preserves 'Raptores' as magical beast type.
Dark elf cavalry on dark steeds. Military and straightforward.
Dark elf chariot. Swift and cruel. 'Carro de Guerra' emphasizes martial purpose.
Standard German term for hydra. Multi-headed monster. Same in Spanish; mythological term.
Harpyien = harpies. Flying monsters in service of dark elves. Standard mythological term.
Direct translation. Fearsome black dragon. Classic and imposing.
Corrupted pegasus. Flying mount for heroes. Pegaso kept as mythological term; Oscuro emphasizes corruption.
Noble dark elf leader. Cruel and cunning. Emphasizes both dark nature and high status.
Priesterin = priestess. Female dark magic user and leader. Sacerdotisa conveys religious authority and magic.
Master of stealth and murder. Deadly hero unit. Direct and menacing; fits dark elf aesthetic.
